what_it_does: This file is a structured interpretive extraction of the back half (slides 16-30) of a capital-raise pitch deck. It records the narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, a large set of categorized visual/design findings with recipes and generator rules, and a partial list of reusable components. It treats the deck as a design substrate and translates observed visual choices into prescriptive system guidance.
observations: The file covers slides p16-p30 and labels the deck's arc as social proof → capital rationale → appendix evidence → contact close.

It contains 43 findings grouped into categories such as gradient-shading-opacity, geometry, type-hierarchy, padding-alignment, color-palette, atom, line-connector, texture, chrome-mode, zone, and sequence.
Each finding includes a name, recipe, evidence slide, emergent boolean, and a generator rule.
Per-slide attention flow entries describe eye-entry points, scan order, and compositional anchors for every slide.
A warm gold (#C8A84B) is the dominant accent, used for names, labels, data figures, bullets, and the persistent V watermark.
The file distinguishes two golds: a lighter 'data gold' and a darker 'title gold' for section labels and subtitles.
It documents a ghosted architectural watermark at 4-8% opacity on most content slides.
It records two chrome modes: DATA MODE (white grid, full chrome) and GALLERY MODE (full-bleed photo, minimal text), plus a CATALOGUE MODE (wordmark top-centre, horizontal rows).
It defines an arrow grammar: right-pointing triangle = forward/capability, left-pointing arrow = inbound result, up/down circles = trends.
It notes a deliberate density reduction through the appendix as a confidence signal.
It includes a components section that is truncated mid-description.
standouts: The arrow-direction grammar that encodes narrative causality (right/left/up/down).
The two-tier gold system separating data figures from title labels.
The full-bleed luxury-photo dividers used as narrative 'breath' and chapter breaks.
The ghosted architectural watermark that differentiates white space from blank white.
The H1 titles written as conversational claims or questions rather than neutral labels.
conventions: Follows a consistent schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator rule.
Uses slide identifiers p16-p30 uniformly across attention flow and findings.
Adopts atomic design vocabulary (atom, component, zone, sequence).
Breaks standard bullet conventions by using a right-pointing gold triangle everywhere.
Breaks pure-white panel conventions by using a warm cream fill (#FAF7EA).
Breaks QR-code placement conventions by embedding codes inside photographs rather than as separate UI elements.
Reserves green and red strictly for directional semantics.
concerns: The components section is truncated, so several component descriptions are incomplete.
Many values (hex codes, opacity percentages, pixel sizes, dash patterns) are approximate inferences from a raster image and may not be exact.
The emergent boolean is not defined, making its basis subjective.
Some categories overlap; for example, gradient-shading-opacity contains panel fills and watermarks that are not gradients or opacity effects.
Generator rules are prescriptive and do not note exceptions or alternative treatments.
The file mixes descriptive observation with normative design instructions without separating evidence from recommendation.
notes: A dense, opinionated design-system extraction of a pitch-deck's second half, strong on generative recipes but truncated in its component catalog.
questions: What is the exact schema relationship between findings, components, and zones?
How were color values and measurements derived (eyedropper, manual estimate, automated extraction)?
What does the truncated components list contain beyond the Person Row and Silhouette Advisory Icon?
How does this file relate to the corresponding part-1 extraction?
Who is the intended consumer: a designer, a developer, or a generative model?
Are the generator rules meant to be strict mandates or illustrative starting points?
purpose_vs_actual: The file does not state an explicit purpose line, but its content is clearly an interpretive extraction of design patterns from slides 16-30; it matches that purpose closely.
apparent_themes: Evidence-forward credibility building in the appendix
Systematic visual grammar with semantic arrows and color restraint
Architectural drafting vocabulary (line-art icons, ghosted plans, circles)
Intentional sparseness as a signal of confidence
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to turn a finished pitch deck into a reusable, generative design system by naming every visual pattern, giving it a measurable recipe, and stating how to reproduce it.
novelty: The combination of per-slide attention-flow narration with generative recipes, an arrow grammar that encodes causality, embedded QR codes treated as in-photo product demos, and the idea of reducing slide density as a confidence signal.
contribution: A richly structured inventory of warm-gold, architectural, luxury-pitch visual patterns tied to specific slides, plus reusable component definitions and generative rules that a fused system could keep as a reference for producing consistent deck layouts.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a structured design extraction of slides 16-30 of a capital-raise pitch deck, documenting narrative arc, per-slide attention flow, visual-system findings with recipes and generator rules, and reusable components for a warm-gold, architectural, luxury-real-estate presentation language.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to translate the back half of a pitch deck into a codified, reusable design system and generative brief.
